source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 807

actions_taken: Onsite medic provided first aid treatment
Injured person evacuated to onshore medical facility in Houma, LA
Injured person received sutures at medical facility in Morgan City, LA
Incident reported to BSEE on 7 March 2023
BSEE office investigation conducted on 28 March 2023
event.summary: On 24 February 2023 at approximately 1115 hours, a Mechanic employed by Helmerich & Payne was attempting to install a hydraulic line on the Pipe Handling Machine (PHM) at MC 807A (Mars TLP). When the combination wrench being used was determined to be too long to access the fitting, the Mechanic decided to modify the wrench by cutting it shorter using a manual hack saw. The wrench was placed in a vise to stabilize it during cutting. As the final cut was made, the Mechanic's weight shifted, causing his hand to come down onto the freshly cut sharp edge of the wrench, resulting in a laceration between the ring and little finger. The Mechanic received first aid onsite and was evacuated to an onshore medical facility where sutures were applied. The Mechanic returned to the platform on 26 February 2023.
recommendations: Ensure pre-job safety and operation meetings adequately identify required tools before work begins
Implement requirement to stop job and reassess risks when new exposures are recognized not covered in JSA
Enhance supervision to ensure workers do not modify tools without approval
Ensure workers discuss tool modifications and risks with supervisors and coworkers before proceeding

threats.name: Worker modifies tool without proper assessment
Lack of communication between worker and supervisor

controls.name: Pre-job safety and operation meeting
controls.barrier_role: Ensure correct tools are identified and gathered before work begins
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent tool modification by identifying proper tools upfront

supporting_text: Pre-job safety and operation meeting proves to be inadequate by not ensuring the correct tools for the job task were gathered
JSA lists 'Gather tools needed for job' as step 2, but the correct tool for the job task was not obtained

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) - Stop and reassess procedure
controls.barrier_role: Require job stoppage and risk reassessment when new exposures are recognized
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control requiring workers to stop work and reassess when new hazards emerge

supporting_text: H&P's Job Safety Analysis (JSA) states at the top, 'If during the task new exposures are recognized which are not covered in the JSA, STOP the job, reassess the risks, and revise the JSA.'

Once it was determined that a shorter wrench was needed, the job should have been stopped, the risks should have been reassessed, and a revision to the JSA should have been made with the supervisor's approval

controls.name: Supervisor oversight and approval of tool modifications
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unauthorized tool modifications through supervisory control
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control requiring supervisor approval before any tool modification

supporting_text: There was no discussion with the supervisor about modifying the wrench or obtaining the proper tool
BSEE's investigation finds lack of supervision to be a probable cause leading up to the incident

controls.name: Worker communication with coworkers and supervisor
controls.barrier_role: Ensure workers communicate about task changes and risks before proceeding
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control requiring communication between workers and supervisors

supporting_text: The IP did not discuss altering the combination wrench with the electrician coworker
The IP's decision to modify the wrench was not discussed with supervisor or with coworker

controls.name: Cut-resistant gloves
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of laceration injury from sharp edges
controls.lod_basis: Personal protective equipment to mitigate consequences of sharp edge contact

supporting_text: The IP was wearing gloves at the time of the incident; however, they were not cut resistant

controls.name: Onsite medical response
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate first aid and medical assessment
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to provide immediate medical response and evacuation

supporting_text: An onsite medic provided first aid treatment to the IP, but determined further treatment was needed at an onshore medical facility
At 1556 hours, the IP was sent onshore to the heliport in Houma, LA

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
